title: Token limits and the context window
description: Input plus requested output must fit the model's context window, and the output is separately capped by max-tokens.
body: Use when prompts grow large or outputs get cut off. The context window bounds input tokens plus generated tokens together; max-tokens caps only the output. Exceeding the window errors or silently drops content, and an output longer than max-tokens is truncated with a length finish-reason. Do not assume a fixed character budget (tokens are not characters), and avoid ignoring the finish-reason. Actions: estimate token counts, reserve headroom for output, and check the finish-reason on every response. Validation signals include a test that an over-window request is caught before sending and that a length-truncated response is flagged. Risk boundary: a truncated structured response acted on as complete corrupts state; treat truncation as failure.
